title: Web: HtmlRenderer component with sandboxed iframe and open-in-new-tab
description:
Add web/src/components/Document/renderers/HtmlRenderer.tsx and wire it into the renderer registry.

Requirements:
1. Renders <iframe src={rawUrl} sandbox="allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-popups" title={label} /> at full available height. rawUrl points at the server raw endpoint from task 75b1ccb8 (/api/issues/:id/documents/:path/raw). When content.commit is set, append ?commit=... so pinned-version links still work.
2. allow-scripts is required for reveal.js; allow-same-origin lets the deck fetch co-located static assets; allow-popups lets in-deck links open.
3. Header toolbar: document label; path; "Open in new tab" button (target=_blank rel=noopener) pointing at rawUrl; content-type badge for parity with Markdown renderer.
4. Skip search highlighting and the history panel — neither applies through an iframe.
5. Add getRawDocumentUrl(issueId, path, commit?) helper to web/src/api/client.ts.
6. Registry selection: content.content_type === "text/html" → HtmlRenderer.

Acceptance:
- Opening a reveal.js .html document through the web UI renders the deck inline with working keyboard navigation and the reveal.js f fullscreen shortcut.
- "Open in new tab" opens the raw HTML standalone.
- End-to-end: works against the ../gf2 repo's docs/presentations/d4851c3d-modem-framework.html.

Depends on: 75b1ccb8 (server raw endpoint) and 42bd50ce (renderer registry). Part of story abfd6016.
